69.reason: [insert points to memory allocated in read(); however, if insertWord is called after lookUp finds no match, this is correct. But note: read() always mallocs, so this free is okay鈥攂ut only if insert is always heap-allocated, which it is. However, the bigger issue is that insertWord is called only when word is new, so count should be 1, not incremented.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [insert points to memory allocated by read(); however, if insertWord is called after lookUp finds no match, this is correct. But note: read() always mallocs, so this free is okay here. However, in lookUp(), when match is found, search is freed 鈥 but what if same string is used elsewhere? Not the main issue. The real problem is that insertWord is only called when new word is added, so freeing insert is acceptable. So maybe not an error here. But the bigger issue is the uninitialized count.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [insert points to memory allocated in read(); however, if insertWord is called after lookUp finds no match, this is correct. But note: read() always mallocs, so this free is okay鈥攂ut only if insert is always heap-allocated, which it is. However, the bigger issue is that insertWord is called only when word is new, so count should be 1, not incremented.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.125 , reason:  [insert points to memory allocated in read(); however, if the same word is inserted multiple times, this leads to double-free when lookUp also calls free(search)]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [insert points to memory allocated in read(); but calling free() on it here and also in lookUp() leads to double-free if insertWord is called after lookUp()]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Attempting to free memory that may not have been allocated by malloc (e.g., static or stack memory), but in this program read() does malloc, so this might be okay鈥攂ut dangerous design]

83.reason: [This free is correct because read() always returns malloc'd memory, and if word exists, we don't need the new copy. So this is okay.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This free is correct because read() always returns malloc'd memory, and if word exists, we don't need the new copy. So this is okay.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.125 , reason:  [search is freed here, but if insertWord is later called with same pointer (which it isn't in this flow), or if read() returns same buffer, could cause issues. More critically, every call to read() returns new malloc'd memory, so every path must free it exactly once 鈥 but design makes that error-prone]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Frees memory that will also be freed in insertWord if not found, causing double-free when word is new]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Same issue: freeing parameter that came from read()'s malloc鈥攖echnically okay here, but inconsistent with typical C practices and error-prone]

101.reason: [Does not handle EOF correctly at start; also, words may be separated by punctuation, digits, or other non-alphabetic characters, not just space/newline鈥攙iolates problem's definition of word as alphabetic sequence]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Does not handle EOF correctly at start; also, words may be separated by punctuation or other non-alphabetic characters, not just space/newline. Problem defines word as sequence of letters only, so non-letter delimiters should end word, but this loop only stops at space or newline.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Does not handle EOF correctly at start; also skips non-alphabetic characters improperly per problem spec]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Does not handle punctuation or non-alphabetic characters correctly per problem definition; only splits on space and newline, but words should be sequences of letters only]
